extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC Primoz attacked with 700m remaining on Col San Martin summit finish, caught breakaway rider MADER Gino in final 20 meters
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC refused to soft-pedal for breakaway sentiment, attacked solo at 700m despite SCHACHMANN neutralizing, prioritizing stage hunting over energy conservation.
INEOS placed two riders (AMADOR, DE PLUS) in 13-rider break but neither survived selection, revealing depth issues against Jumbo's protected leader.
BENNETT Sam training specifically for Milan-San Remo Poggio accelerations by riding breakaway climbs, not pure time-cut survival riding.
SCHACHMANN marked ROGLIC's accelerations but couldn't counter-attack, defending GC rather than racing for stage despite being 52 seconds down.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: ruthlessly prioritized third stage win with late attack despite already controlling GC, suggesting peak form for stage hunting
rider_signals.MADER Gino: positive: survived all-day breakaway and nearly held off GC contenders on steep summit despite being caught 20m from line
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: negative: could only follow ROGLIC's moves rather than initiate, defensive tactics suggest resigned to second place 52 seconds back
rider_signals.HAMILTON Lucas: positive: finished fourth on summit behind only three GC riders, unexpected climbing form from DSM leader
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: deliberately stayed in early break for Milan-San Remo training despite being sprinter, tactical preparation rather than GC weakness
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: positive: finished in chase group on mountain summit, showing climbing range beyond classics specialist profile
